NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: Physical devices and systems within the organisation are inventoried
Mapping_Rationale: Both controls require a maintained register of all assets — NIST focuses on physical/logical devices; ISO 27001 A.8.1 covers all information assets including data and services
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart maintains a register covering: ordering platform server, CCTV, staff devices, payment terminal, and cloud backup storage
Gap_or_Observation: QuickMart's asset register is in CSV format — should be reviewed and updated quarterly per ISO 27001 requirement

NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: Software platforms and applications within the organisation are inventoried
Mapping_Rationale: Extends ID.AM-1 to software — ISO 27001 A.8.1 encompasses both hardware and software assets in scope for the ISMS
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart inventories: ordering web application, inventory management system, Splunk SIEM, OS versions on all servers
Gap_or_Observation: Software versions should be documented with patch status to support A.12.6 (Technical Vulnerability Management)

QuickMart_Application: and audited for authorised devices and users
column11: Both controls govern the full lifecycle of user access — issuance, management, and revocation. ISO A.9.2 maps directly to NIST's credential lifecycle management
column12: QuickMart assigns unique credentials at onboarding; revokes within 24 hours of staff departure; admin accounts reviewed monthly
column13: Access review frequency should be formally documented — current process is informal and not recorded

NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: Access permissions and authorisations are managed incorporating the principles of least privilege and separation of duties
Mapping_Rationale: Least privilege and separation of duties are core tenets of both NIST PR.AC-4 and ISO A.9.1; both require formal policy and role-based access control
QuickMart_Application: Delivery staff: read-only order access. Store manager: full admin. Finance: payment records only. No cross-role access permitted
Gap_or_Observation: Separation of duties should be formally documented in an access matrix — currently enforced in practice but not in writing

Equivalent_ISO27001_Control_Name: Policy on the Use of Cryptographic Controls
Mapping_Rationale: ISO A.10.1 directly governs encryption policies — NIST PR.DS-1 requires data-at-rest protection which is typically achieved through encryption
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart encrypts customer database at rest using AES-256; payment card data stored in encrypted format on compliant payment gateway
Gap_or_Observation: Encryption key management policy not yet documented — required for full ISO A.10.1 compliance

NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
Mapping_Rationale: ISO A.12.4 requires event logs and regular review — NIST DE.CM-1 requires continuous network monitoring; both aim to detect anomalous activity
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart uses Splunk to ingest and review auth.log, system logs, and failed login events; alerts configured for repeated failed logins
Gap_or_Observation: Log review is currently manual and daily — automated alerting rules should be expanded to cover data exfiltration patterns

NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: A baseline of network operations and expected data flows for users and systems is established and managed
Mapping_Rationale: ISO A.12.1 requires documented operational procedures — NIST DE.AE-1 requires a baseline of normal operations to detect deviations
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart has documented normal operating hours, expected login times for staff, and flagged after-hours admin access as anomalous
Gap_or_Observation: Formal baseline documentation does not yet exist — should be created and stored in the operations runbook

NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: Response plan is executed during or after an incident
Mapping_Rationale: ISO A.16.1 requires a defined incident management procedure — NIST RS.RP-1 requires executing a response plan; both govern the organisational response to security events
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart follows a 5-step incident response procedure: Identify, Contain, Eradicate, Recover, Report; all incidents documented with severity and timeline
Gap_or_Observation: Response plan exists but has not been formally tested via a tabletop exercise — testing recommended before go-live

NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: Incidents are reported consistent with established criteria
Mapping_Rationale: ISO A.16.1 includes reporting requirements — NIST RS.CO-2 addresses incident reporting criteria; both ensure incidents are escalated and communicated appropriately
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart's incident procedure defines: P1 (Critical) reported to management within 1 hour; P2 within 4 hours; P3 within 24 hours
Gap_or_Observation: Regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., DPDP Act 2023 breach notification) not yet incorporated into the response plan

NIST_CSF_Subcategory_Description: Recovery plan is executed during or after a cybersecurity incident
Mapping_Rationale: ISO A.17.1 requires business continuity planning covering information security — NIST RC.RP-1 requires execution of recovery plans; both govern restoration of operations after an incident
QuickMart_Application: QuickMart's recovery plan includes: restore from latest daily backup, switch to manual order processing if platform is down, notify customers via SMS if data is affected
Gap_or_Observation: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) have not been formally defined — should be documented and agreed by management
